methodology: Every item is taken on a minimum of 2 real camping trips before review — no backyard or hotel-room tests.
Tents are set up in wind and rain; sleeping bags are rated against actual temperature performance, not manufacturer claims.
Pack weight is measured on our own scale; volume claims are verified by packing the listed capacity in real gear.
We note pack-out durability: gear that fails after one season is marked accordingly and removed from recommendations.
Budget picks are taken as seriously as premium picks — we name the cheapest option that doesn't compromise safety or comfort.
Affiliate links go to lowest price across REI, Amazon, and manufacturer direct; we note price-match policies.

newsletter.title: The CampDeck weekly
newsletter.subtitle: Practical picks and straight answers from CampDeck, once a week. No spam.

homepage.subhead: Reviews of tents, sleeping bags, backpacks, camp stoves, and outdoor gear tested on real trips. We carry what we recommend, sleep in what we review, and cook on what we rate. No glamping gear. No Instagram setups.
homepage.trustLine: Gear tested on real trips, in real weather. Bought at retail. No brand partnerships.

about.headline: Why CampDeck exists
about.body: Outdoor gear reviews are dominated by people who test equipment in ideal conditions and write up vague impressions. We camp in rain, sleep in cold, and cook on wind-exposed ridgelines. If gear fails in those conditions we say so. If it's worth every dollar we say that too.
